It is native to Tajikistan and is a member of the genus Zeravschania, which was previously classified in the lily family, Liliaceae. This genus is a monotypic genus, meaning it contains only a single species, Zeravschania pastinacifolia. The classification of Zeravschania pastinacifolia within the Asparagaceae family is based on phylogenetic studies and molecular analysis. The correct classification of this species is a subject of ongoing research in botany.

The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to help in the treatment of various health conditions, including pain management and wound healing.
